Execution Sequence of Triggers and Operations in Web Applications

Each time the WRD sends an HTTP request to the application server, a sequence of triggers and operations are activated.

The WRD sends an HTTP request to the application server, which activates the activates the USYSHTTP, and the following sequence of triggers and operations are activated:

Execution Sequence Trigger or Operation Description
1 preRequest trigger If you defined an application shell for the Uniface Server, this is the first trigger to be fired. It can be used for authentication.
2 Init operation You can define this operation in the server page to initialize each HTTP request.
3 preActivate trigger This server page trigger can be used for getting state information.
4 exec operation This trigger is fired if the web request is loading a server page. This can include a URL for a USP or DSP, or loading a DSP into a DspContainer widget.

If the code contains a webget (USP only) or webload (DSP only), it calls the validation triggers of database fields, and calls the detail trigger of the submitted button.
4 Operations If the request is calling an operation in a server page, the operation is executed.

In USPs, if the action attribute of the <form> tag is defined with an operation, for example, my_usp.myop, this operation is called when the server page is submitted.

For example, <form method="post" action="my_usp.myop" onsubmit="return uSubmit(this)">.

If the code contains a webget (USP only) or webload (DSP only), it calls the validation triggers of database fields, and calls the detail trigger of the submitted button.
4 Attach operation This operation is executed instead of the exec operation when the following conditions are met:
  • A DSP instance name is assigned to a DspContainer widget
  • The contained DSP instance is already available
  • The Attach operation has been defined. You can define this operation in the contained DSP
5 postActivate trigger This server page trigger can be used for setting state information.
6 postRequest trigger This trigger exists in an application shell if you defined one for the Uniface Server.
7 Cleanup operation You can define this operation in the server page for cleaning up after each HTTP request.
Note: The Cleanup operation cannot access the Web context information contained in $webinfo.
Related Links
  • Using Uniface Authentication
  • State Management Mechanisms

    The stateless nature of the HTTP protocol means that Uniface server pages are stateless and must explicitly implement state management using one or more of the available state management mechanisms.

  • Calling Operations on Static Server Pages

    You can call operations on a static server page by extending the URL to include the operation name, or by specifying the operation in the Action URL attribute of the <form> element of a static server page. This provides a mechanism for sharing state information between USPs.

  • USYSHTTP

    The USYSHTTP component is a global web application request processor that handles all web requests sent to a Uniface Server via the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD).
